title: Math and science foundation flow
description: Build the mathematical and probabilistic base required to understand modern ML with rigor rather than pattern matching.

stages.title: Linear algebra and calculus for model mechanics
stages.objective: Learn the vector, matrix, and gradient concepts behind representation learning and optimization.
stages.feedbackLoop: After each study block, derive one update rule or matrix identity from memory and connect it to a model component.

stages.title: Probability, statistics, and optimization
stages.objective: Build the scientific language used to reason about uncertainty, generalization, and learning objectives.
stages.feedbackLoop: End each unit by writing a short note explaining how the math shows up in evaluation, fitting, or experimental design.

title: Scientific computing and data systems flow
description: Learn the practical computing substrate that makes MLE work repeatable and production-ready.

stages.title: Python ML tooling and classical pipelines
stages.objective: Master preprocessing, pipeline composition, evaluation, and experiment hygiene for structured data.
stages.feedbackLoop: For every pipeline you build, record assumptions, leakage risks, ablations, and reproducibility details.

stages.title: Model training and orchestration infrastructure
stages.objective: Understand the runtime side of modern ML systems from training loops to workflow orchestration.
stages.feedbackLoop: After each systems exercise, write the failure mode you would monitor first in production.

title: ML 101 source flow
description: Move from quantitative basics into disciplined machine learning habits.

stages.title: Ground the mechanics first
stages.objective: Use math and statistics to understand why baseline models behave the way they do.
stages.feedbackLoop: Re-explain every baseline result as a consequence of assumptions, not just leaderboard score.

stages.title: Practice evaluation and reproducibility
stages.objective: Treat problem framing, splits, and metrics as the heart of the discipline.
stages.feedbackLoop: Keep a running error-analysis log and rewrite your experiment plan after every failure pattern you discover.

title: History and context flow
description: Use open course materials as anchors for how the field moved from symbolic thinking to representation-heavy systems.

stages.title: Compare scientific eras through actual course material
stages.objective: Read the field through its mathematical and implementation demands, not just hype cycles.
stages.feedbackLoop: After each milestone, write one critique explaining which historical weakness still survives in modern AI systems.

title: Classical ML source flow
description: Build the baseline modeling toolkit every strong ML engineer should command before overusing deep learning.

stages.title: Master structured prediction and evaluation
stages.objective: Learn model selection, preprocessing, calibration, and feature pipelines for real tabular and sparse problems.
stages.feedbackLoop: Maintain a baseline ledger that explains why each stronger model earns its additional complexity.

title: Statistical inference and probabilistic modeling flow
description: Strengthen the science side of ML through inference, uncertainty, and probabilistic reasoning.

stages.title: Inference, uncertainty, and experiments
stages.objective: Understand confidence, posterior reasoning, and why evaluation is a scientific act as much as an engineering one.
stages.feedbackLoop: After every statistical exercise, write what conclusion is justified, what is not, and what additional evidence you still need.

title: Deep learning source flow
description: Bridge theory, code, and architecture using openly licensed deep learning materials.

stages.title: Learn deep learning with code and derivations
stages.objective: Combine a broad open textbook with concrete framework tutorials so theory stays attached to implementation.
stages.feedbackLoop: Break and fix at least one training run per module and document the exact diagnostic signal.

title: Vision and multimodal source flow
description: Bridge visual representation learning, structured perception tasks, and multimodal retrieval using open textbook and framework materials.

stages.title: Perception architectures and evaluation
stages.objective: Learn how visual features, localization tasks, and shift-aware metrics change what good modeling looks like.
stages.feedbackLoop: After each vision exercise, inspect at least one failure visually and explain whether the root cause is representation, data, metric choice, or deployment context.

stages.title: Multimodal retrieval and product grounding
stages.objective: Treat image-text systems as embedding, retrieval, and evaluation pipelines rather than just bigger models.
stages.feedbackLoop: Keep a failure ledger for cross-modal retrieval mistakes and force every fix to justify why it improves user-relevant relevance instead of just a proxy metric.

title: Production ML source flow
description: Move from experiments to durable systems with open infrastructure references.

stages.title: Track, package, and operate models
stages.objective: Tie experiments, lineage, release criteria, and deployment control into one repeatable lifecycle.
stages.feedbackLoop: For each systems artifact, write the rollback trigger and the first metric you would put on an alert.

title: LLM and transformer systems flow
description: Use open transformer and framework materials to study retrieval, adaptation, and evaluation.

stages.title: Transformers and adaptation
stages.objective: Learn tokenization, attention, transfer learning, and fine-tuning through openly licensed materials.
stages.feedbackLoop: After each lesson, explain one transformer failure by tracing the data path instead of blaming the model abstractly.

title: Sequential decision-making source flow
description: Use open math, deep learning, and framework references to study sequential feedback, policy optimization, and safer rollout discipline.

stages.title: MDPs, returns, and instability
stages.objective: Learn the abstractions behind sequential learning and why optimization becomes fragile under feedback loops and delayed reward.
stages.feedbackLoop: For every RL concept, write one paragraph on what assumption would break first in a messy product environment.

stages.title: Bandits, offline evidence, and deployment safety
stages.objective: Study the practical forms of sequential learning most likely to appear in product ranking, recommendation, or decision support systems.
stages.feedbackLoop: End each unit by stating the weakest piece of evidence in your rollout plan and what additional data you would demand before launch.

title: Reliable frontier ML flow
description: Use the final track to synthesize robustness, systems, and applied research judgment.

stages.title: Operate at the frontier with discipline
stages.objective: Treat advanced ML as a combination of modeling, science, infrastructure, and governance.
stages.feedbackLoop: Every capstone should end with an adversarial review that scores evidence, uncertainty, cost, and operational risk.
